narrative: hbc-phase-gate has strong fundamentals — correct intelligence placement (FILE/CONTENT/METRIC in script, QUALITY in LLM), well-designed customization surface with per-phase checklist overrides, and a solid delta-tracking pattern for re-evaluations. However, the decision log contradicts the implemented headless mode (a critical audit-trail gap), the on_complete hook fires unconditionally (latent bug), P4-07 validates file existence rather than gate status (logic gap), and the activation steps are over-prescribed. The design is architecturally sound but has real bugs to fix before the skill can be trusted in integrated pipelines.
false_positives: P1 prepass flagged 1-gate-checklist.md, 2-gate-log.md, 2-gate.md as missing stage files — these are regex false positives extracting fragments from phase-N-gate-*.md patterns in SKILL.md prose. No actual dangling references.
S1 path-standards: all 11 findings are from .analysis/2026-05-26T160200/ (previous analysis artifacts), not skill source files. No path issues in SKILL.md or customize.toml.

broken.title: Decision log contradicts implemented headless mode
broken.description: Decision log records 'No headless mode' as a settled decision, but SKILL.md implements a full ## Headless Mode section with JSON schema. Agents resuming from the decision log would believe headless is out of scope.
broken.action: Append a new session entry to .decision-log.md recording the headless mode addition, the rationale, and which prior decision it overrides. Do not edit the old entry (append-only).

broken.title: on_complete hook fires regardless of gate status
broken.description: on_complete fires unconditionally — a team configuring 'invoke hbc-traceability' would trigger traceability update even after a FAILED gate, causing unintended downstream side effects.
broken.action: Gate the hook on status: fire on PASSED only; for FAILED/WARNING, present as suggested next step after user resolves failures.

broken.title: P4-07 checks file existence, not gate outcome
broken.description: P4-07 'All previous gates PASSED' uses FILE type, which only checks that gate report files exist — not that they contain PASSED status. A project with FAILED gate reports still passes P4-07.
broken.action: Change P4-07 type to CONTENT with criteria matching the gate report's status field (e.g. 'overall_status: PASSED' or '**Status:** PASSED').

broken.title: .decision-log.md misplaced in references/
broken.description: references/ is for prompt content carved out of SKILL.md. The decision log is a build-time workspace artifact and should be at skill root as a peer of SKILL.md and customize.toml.
broken.action: Move to src/hbc-phase-gate/.decision-log.md. Remove empty references/ directory.

opportunities.title: QUALITY item handling in headless mode is undefined
opportunities.description: Headless JSON includes pending_llm count but doesn't specify whether QUALITY items are evaluated (LLM judges without user prompts) or skipped. Ambiguity makes headless status untrustworthy for automation.
opportunities.action: Add one sentence: 'QUALITY items are evaluated headlessly using the same LLM judgment logic — no user prompts are issued.'

opportunities.title: On Activation Steps 2-4 over-prescribed
opportunities.description: Steps 2 (execute prepend), 3 (load persistent facts), and 4 (load config) are single-line instructions the LLM handles natively. Five numbered steps for activation is procedure-padding.
opportunities.action: Collapse Steps 2-4 into one sentence. Keep Step 1 (exact script invocation) and Step 5 (phase inference logic with agent-context mapping).

opportunities.title: P3-03/P3-04 have no remediation path for artifact-less projects
opportunities.description: P3-03 expects coverage-report file, P3-04 expects test-results file. Both required. Many projects run tests without persisting results. No skill_to_create or hint on how to generate expected artifacts.
opportunities.action: Add hint column or documentation_hint values: P3-03 hint for exporting coverage report, P3-04 hint for exporting test results.

opportunities.title: Missing unit tests for evaluate-gate-checklist.py
opportunities.description: 270-line script with checklist parsing, glob resolution, regex matching, and metric extraction has no tests. Empty tests/ directory signals unfulfilled intent.
opportunities.action: Create tests for parse_checklist, evaluate_file, evaluate_content, evaluate_metric functions. Or remove empty directory if tests aren't being maintained.

opportunities.title: Near-match discovery should be in script, not LLM-executed find
opportunities.description: LLM runs find command for FILE failures. This is deterministic work — extend evaluate-gate-checklist.py to include near-match candidates in JSON evidence field.
opportunities.action: Add --near-match flag to evaluate-gate-checklist.py or integrate into existing FILE evaluation. Remove explicit find command from SKILL.md.

opportunities.title: gate_report_template not exposed as customizable scalar
opportunities.description: assets/gate-report-template.md is referenced by bare path in SKILL.md but not exposed in customize.toml. Teams needing branded/extended report layout must fork the skill.
opportunities.action: Add gate_report_template scalar to customize.toml. Change SKILL.md reference to {workflow.gate_report_template}.

opportunities.title: First-run delta is semantically misleading
opportunities.description: On first gate run (no prior results), every item appears as NEW in delta. Headless callers may misinterpret all-NEW delta as change signal.
opportunities.action: Add conditional: if prior_results empty, skip delta table and note 'First evaluation — no prior results.' In headless JSON, add is_first_run: true.

opportunities.title: Gate log lacks quick-scan status header
opportunities.description: Gate log is append-only delta tables. No top-level 'Current status: PASSED/FAILED' header for quick scanning across multiple re-runs.
opportunities.action: Add instruction: maintain two-line header (Current status + Last evaluated) at top of gate log, updated on each run.

opportunities.title: document_output_language not referenced in gate report
opportunities.description: Gate report and checklists have no {document_output_language} substitution. Report is always in template language regardless of config.
opportunities.action: Add {document_output_language} to gate report template header. Note in Evaluate Gate that report is produced in configured language.

opportunities.title: Delta table construction could be a script
opportunities.description: Delta computation (join prior vs current, classify fixed/regressed/new/unchanged) is pure data manipulation. ~80-120 token savings per run.
opportunities.action: Optional: create generate-gate-log-entry.py script for delta table. Low priority given small token savings.

opportunities.title: Headless Mode section placement interrupts standard flow
opportunities.description: ## Headless Mode appears between Overview and Conventions, breaking standard BMad order (Overview → Conventions → On Activation).
opportunities.action: Move ## Headless Mode after ## Evaluate Gate or before ## On Complete.

opportunities.title: Gate report template HTML comments are author instructions
opportunities.description: HTML comments like '<!-- For each FAIL item... -->' may appear in rendered output if LLM writes template verbatim.
opportunities.action: Remove HTML comment instructions. LLM will produce correct content from section headings.

opportunities.title: Revision History table in template is obsolete
opportunities.description: Template includes empty Revision History table. Gate log now handles re-evaluation history, making this dead placeholder.
opportunities.action: Replace with one-liner linking to phase-N-gate-log.md.

opportunities.title: customize.toml persistent_facts and on_complete lack comments
opportunities.description: persistent_facts has no guidance on what to append; on_complete has no guidance on valid values. Other scalars have comments.
opportunities.action: Add brief comments explaining usage and valid values.

strengths: Intelligence placement is textbook — FILE/CONTENT/METRIC checks are fully deterministic in script, QUALITY items pass through to LLM judgment
customize.toml design is well-considered: four per-phase checklist scalars, gate_mode intentionally excluded with documented rationale
Delta comparison pattern (prior_results → fixed/regressed/new/unchanged) adds real value for re-evaluations
Headless JSON schema is well-formed with required_failed array and gate_mode in return
Variable substitution via --var flags keeps string interpolation in the script, not the LLM
Description format is correct with quoted bilingual trigger phrases
recommendations: Fix the 4 broken items first (decision log update, on_complete gating, P4-07 type change, decision-log location)
Then address the 3 high opportunities (headless QUALITY semantics, activation collapse, P3 remediation hints)
Unit tests for evaluate-gate-checklist.py should be written — 270 lines of parsing logic deserves coverage
Near-match discovery integration into the script would eliminate a shell tool call from LLM turns
